This module is a case study on data stewardship in practice, built around the Global Health Engineering (GHE) group at ETH Zurich. There is no lecture this week; you will read an interview, watch a talk, and reflect on whether and how the approach could apply to your own research context.


::: {.cell}

:::



::: {.cell}

:::


## {{< fa bullseye >}} Learning Objectives


::: {.cell}
1. Learners can summarise the data stewardship model used by the Global Health Engineering group at ETH Zurich.
2. Learners can explain what "open by default" means in a research group context and identify two organizational factors that enable or resist it.
3. Learners can critique an existing data management strategy and propose adaptations for their own research context.
:::


## {{< fa book >}} Readings

{{< fa book >}} Read @wilson2017 "Good enough practices in scientific computing".

## {{< fa code >}} Homework assignment

[Due date:]{.highlight-yellow} June 12, 2026

Complete the case-study assignments on the course website first, then complete the homework in your Posit Cloud project for Module 3.

-   [Assignment 1](../assignments/md-03/am-03-2-case-study.qmd): Case study, Data stewardship at ETH GHE
-   [Assignment 2](../assignments/md-03/am-03-1-bookmarks.qmd): Support yourself with bookmarks

Then complete the homework programming assignment that is in your project folder for Module 3 on [Posit Cloud]({{< var platforms.posit-cloud >}}content/all).

- hw-data-stewardship.qmd

## {{< fa pen-clip >}} Learning reflection

Complete the learning reflection that is in your project folder for Module 3 by [**June 12, 2026**]{.highlight-yellow} on [Posit Cloud]({{< var platforms.posit-cloud >}}content/all).
